mutations.description: Accept invalid watchdog timeout or window bounds.
mutations.find:
driver == NULL || wdt == NULL || timeout_ticks < UINT16_C(2) ||
    timeout_ticks > WATCHDOG_WINDOW_MAX_TIMEOUT_TICKS ||
    window_open_ticks == 0u || window_open_ticks >= timeout_ticks

mutations.description: Discard the reset cause observed during initialization.
Do not clear the watchdog reset cause while configuring WDT0.
Fail to stop WDT0 before changing its timeout and window configuration.
Write the hardware feed key before the configured window opens.
Treat the exact window-open counter as too early.
Use an invalid hardware key while feeding WDT0.
Proceed with normal feed handling after the hardware reset latch is set.
Allow a feed before reset recovery has been acknowledged.
Restore a disabled interrupt state after a successful feed.
Return no event even after poll observes a watchdog reset.
Observe a reset without latching it for recovery.
Permit recovery configuration without a detected watchdog reset.
Allow reset recovery before the reset-detected event is consumed.
Reconfigure WDT0 even though its reset cause is absent.
Mark recovery complete without restoring the watchdog configuration.
Keep the driver in reset-pending state after WDT0 is reconfigured.
Fail to publish the recovery acknowledgement event.
Return an event without clearing the one-event latch.
Restore interrupts as disabled after consuming a watchdog event.
